name: signal-ledger
summary: Capture every run as a four-tuple-stamped signal. record/signals.jsonl is the append-only truth; state/os.db is the rebuildable projection. First component of the data layer.

admission.note: Candidate. NOT in harness/manifest.json and NOT admitted. Promotion is a human step.

input.description: A hook payload (stdin JSON) or an explicit signal record.

output.description: One appended signal record + the in-memory result.

provenanceMapping.run: per-session today; resolves to per-tool-call id when capture goes per-tool
provenanceMapping.call: the monotonic signal index n (global order)
provenanceMapping.branch: git branch of the working tree
provenanceMapping.harnessVersion: harness/manifest.json harnessVersion — which version of the definition was in force
provenanceMapping.codeSha: git rev-parse --short HEAD — the exact code the row was produced under

invariants: record/signals.jsonl is the source of truth (append-only; supersede, never edit).
state/os.db is rebuildable from record/signals.jsonl and is gitignored.
Truth-first write ordering: append the durable record before the projection.
Nothing fails silently: a failed write is recorded to state/signal-drops.jsonl.
The per-log index n is gapless 1..N — gaps are a detectable completeness failure.
Every signal carries the four-tuple (session, run, call, branch).
Every signal records the harnessVersion + codeSha it was captured under (versioning meets traceability).
